project_type: C++20 Enterprise Desktop Security Platform (CMake-based static libraries)
build_system: CMake 3.22. Modules are static libraries. Dependencies are vendored in third_party/ as header-only libs (spdlog, nlohmann/json, doctest) plus a sqlite3 stub. IMPORTANT: Many modules have CMakeLists.txt referencing 'src/placeholder.cpp' but the actual source files exist with different names. The build is currently BROKEN at cmake configure stage because about 10 modules reference non-existent placeholder.cpp.
test_framework: doctest (header-only, tests/test_main.cpp defines DOCTEST_CONFIG_IMPLEMENT_WITH_MAIN). Tests are in tests/ directory with subdirs matching module structure (core/, database/).
verification_instructions: 1. Run cmake configure. 2. Run cmake --build. 3. Run ctest. All must pass.
snapshot_or_generated_files: build/generated/include/threatone/version.h is generated by cmake Version.cmake. Not checked in.
setup_instructions: No external dependencies to install. All deps vendored. Just cmake -B build -DENABLE_QT=OFF && cmake --build build
environment_constraints: INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network - cannot install packages. GCC 11.5, CMake 3.22 available. No OpenSSL/libsodium for real crypto - must implement SHA256 from scratch or use a header-only implementation. No YARA library available - must stub the integration interface. pthread available for threading.
contribution_requirements: Code must be production-quality C++20. Use existing patterns: Result<T,E> for error handling, ModuleLogger for logging, EventBus for events, ServiceLocator for DI. Follow namespace conventions (ThreatOne::Scanner). All new code must compile and tests must pass.
key_patterns: Result<T, Core::Error> for fallible operations. ModuleLogger for per-module logging. EventBus::publish for events. SecurityEvent for threat notifications. Model<T> CRTP for database entities. Header files in include/scanner/, implementation in src/. IScanEngine interface already defines ScanType, ScanStatus, ScanConfig, ScanResult.
directory_structure: Root CMakeLists.txt adds 25+ subdirectories. Each module has include/<module>/ for headers and src/ for implementation. Third-party vendored in third_party/ (spdlog, nlohmann/json, doctest, sqlite3 stub). Tests in tests/ with test_main.cpp and subdirectories per module. cmake/ has CompilerOptions, Dependencies, Version, QtHelpers.
